UX Case Study · 2025–2026

The future of storytelling

A full product design walkthrough of Nucanon an AI character companion app built with an Indian cultural lens.

Role
Product Designer
Platform
iOS & Android
Duration
~4 months
Tools
Figma
01 Overview

What is
Nucanon?

Nucanon is an AI-powered storytelling and character companion app built for the Indian market. Users can chat with AI personas from mythological figures to anime heroes or create their own characters with deep customisation.

The product blends the rising global demand for interactive AI companions with a distinctly Indian cultural identity: multi-language support, locally relevant characters (Priya, historical figures like Steve Jobs reimagined), and a pricing structure tuned for the Indian consumer.

My role spanned the full design process from research and information architecture through high-fidelity screens, a cohesive design system, and handoff-ready specs.

11+
Unique screens designed across the core user journey
3
Languages supported English, Hindi, Tamil from day one
2
Auth flows (email OTP & mobile OTP) for frictionless onboarding
02 Problem Space

The challenge

"Global AI companion apps like Character.AI ignore Indian users the language, the characters, and the cultural context. Nucanon had to feel built for India, not just translated."

🌐
No Indian cultural hook
Existing products are dominated by Western anime, pop culture, and English-only experiences. Indian mythology, Bollywood, and regional languages were absent.
🔐
Onboarding friction
Most competitors require social login (Google/Apple) which many Indian users distrust or don't have. A lightweight OTP-first flow was essential.
🤖
Creator ownership
Users should feel like authors, not just consumers. The character creation system needed depth backstory, personality, voice tone without overwhelming newcomers.
💎
Sustainable monetisation
A freemium model required careful gating showing value before paywalls, offering yearly savings (−19%), and never blocking core character browsing.

04 Research & Insights

What we learned

Before designing, I mapped competitive products (Character.AI, Replika, Chai) and identified what Indian users found missing. Key patterns emerged.

01
OTP is king in India. Most target users preferred phone/email OTP over social logins faster, more trusted, no Google account dependency.
02
Creators want control. Users who build bots on Character.AI feel limited by shallow forms. They want backstory, tone, daily hooks not just name + greeting.
03
Discoverability is broken. Existing apps surface characters poorly. Tags, format, and language filters together solve a real pain point for non-English speakers.
04
Voice messages matter. Audio-first communication is a pattern in Indian apps (WhatsApp, ShareChat). Building voice input/output into chat felt native.
05
Freemium gates must be visible but not hostile. Indian users are price-sensitive. The upgrade prompt works better as a soft nudge with tangible savings (₹2280/yr).
06
Characters need cultural hooks. Including Priyanka Chopra, historical Indian figures, and multilingual support signalled a product built for India not just translated to India.
05 User Flow

Core journeys

Two primary flows drive the entire product. New user onboarding had to be under 2 minutes; character creation had to feel rewarding, not exhausting.

06 Design Decisions

Why it looks
the way it does

01

Dark-first design with a single coral accent

The dark palette isn't just aesthetic it reduces eye strain during long chat sessions (AI apps get 20–40 min average sessions) and makes character artwork pop. A single coral/salmon red (#F05757) accent handles all interactive states: CTAs, active tabs, progress, selections. This means zero visual ambiguity if it's red, you can tap it.

I deliberately avoided the purple-on-dark cliché common in AI apps. The warm coral reads as approachable and energetic more companion, less cold chatbot.

Color System
Primary
#F05757
Background
#0E0E0E
Surface
#161616
Muted Text
#888888
Interactive States
Start Chat
Edit Character
02

Toggle-based auth not a tab bar

The login screen uses a pill-toggle (Mobile Number / Email ID) rather than separate screens or tabs. This keeps the mental model simple one login page, two methods and reduces navigation depth by one level. The active state uses the coral pill, matching the system accent.

OTP input uses 6 individual character boxes for clear feedback. Error state turns boxes coral-outlined with red warning text no modal, no disruption to flow.

Login Toggle
Mobile Number Email ID
OTP Input Default
4
5
2
OTP Input Error
4
5
2
1
0
9
Invalid code. Please try again.
03

Progress bar over step numbering in character creation

The 4-step character creation form uses a thin progress bar not "Step 2 of 4" labels. The reason: the bar is motivating (you can see the end approaching), while explicit numbers can create abandonment anxiety on step 1.

Fields that needed improvement were flagged inline with a coral (Need Improvement) label a rare production design choice that acknowledged the product's early state without hiding it.

Step 1 of 4
Step 2 of 4
Step 3 of 4
Step 4 of 4 Done
In-form Feedback
Gender * (Need Improvement)
04

Tags as interactive pills not dropdowns

Both character creation and the explore filter use pill tags instead of multi-select dropdowns. This is intentional: pills let users see all options at once, select/deselect with a single tap, and feel the selection immediately (coral fill = selected).

Three taxonomies Format, Genre, Personality give just enough structure to make filtering powerful without becoming overwhelming.

Format
Anime Comics Gaming Music
Genres
Action Adventure Comedy Drama
Personality
Bossy Kind Creative Shy
05

Character intro screen before the first message

Instead of dropping users directly into a chat, a character intro screen shows the persona's avatar, tagline, description, tags, and a greeting message all before you start. This creates an anticipation ritual that makes the first chat feel intentional, not accidental.

Two CTAs: "Start Chat" (primary) and "Edit Character" (ghost, only visible to the creator). The hierarchy is clear the story comes first.

🦹
Super Man
The hero who never stops believing.
A loyal AI companion built to support and guide you. Calm, reliable, and always ready to help.
Anime Super Power Hollywood
Start Chat
Edit Character
07 Retrospective

What I'd do
differently

🧪
Test the character creation depth earlier
The 4-step form is detailed by design, but depth can become friction. I'd prototype the creation flow with 5-8 real Indian users early to identify which fields cause drop-off. The "(Need Improvement)" flags in the designs suggest some fields were already identified as weak testing would validate which to remove or reframe.
📱
Design for low-end Android first
The dark, gradient-heavy design is visually strong, but heavy gradients can stutter on low-RAM Android devices (a large portion of the Indian market). I'd add a performance budget from the start and consider adaptive rendering for lower-end devices.
🗣
Hindi & Tamil UI copy from day one
All design screens are in English. Given the core thesis a product with an "Indian touch" I should have designed at least one screen set in Hindi from the start to surface layout issues (Hindi text is often 30–40% longer than English equivalents).
📊
Define success metrics per screen
A stronger portfolio case study connects every design decision to a measurable outcome. For future projects, I'd attach hypotheses to each screen: "We believe showing the yearly savings clearly will increase PRO conversion by X%" making the design choices easier to evaluate post-launch.
08 Next Steps

What comes next

The current design covers the core loop. These are the features and improvements queued for the next design sprint.

01

Interactive Stories Launched design

The "Interactive Stories are on their way" placeholder screen has a "Notify Me" CTA. The next sprint designs the actual story reader branching narrative paths, character cards mid-story, and save/resume.

02

Voice Message UI polish

The waveform visualizer in chat needs a proper design pass playback scrubbing, speaker identification, and seamless inline rendering within the chat bubble.

03

Creator Analytics dashboard

Public character creators will want to track engagement chat counts, likes, daily active users of their characters. A lightweight creator analytics screen is planned for the Character tab.

04

Accessibility audit

The dark theme with muted grays may fail contrast minimums for some text sizes. A full WCAG 2.1 audit especially for body copy and tag labels is needed before launch.

05

Notification design system

Currently, the notifications screen shows an empty state ("No Notifications Yet"). The full notification taxonomy new message, character liked, story update, PRO expiry needs to be designed and templated.
